headline: The player corpus is 78.1% intermediate — a coaching goldmine hiding in plain sight
insight: Of 494 players detected, 78.1% are intermediate, 4.9% advanced, 1.2% beginner. Intermediate players are the most valuable coaching customer — engaged enough to pay, frustrated enough to need help.

headline: 'Overhead' is the highest-quality shot in the corpus — brands aren't there yet
insight: Across 1982 analyzed shots, 'overhead' has the highest composite score (8.6/10, n=5). This shot type generates the most audience engagement. Any brand that owns this moment owns pickleball's best highlight.

headline: Kitchen mastery is the #1 predictor of skill level — data proves what coaches always suspected
insight: Kitchen mastery by skill: {'intermediate': 6.97, 'advanced_intermediate': 7.0, 'advanced': 7.75, 'unknown': 6.0, 'advanced intermediate': 7.5}. 'advanced' players avg 7.75/10. Creativity follows: {'intermediate': 3.01, 'advanced_intermediate': 5.0, 'advanced': 6.2, 'unknown': 3.8, 'advanced intermediate': 3.0, 'beginner': 6.0}. This is the coaching thesis proven at scale.

headline: Average rally is 16.1 shots — but 59.7% go epic (12+), creating outsized viral value
insight: From 124 clips: avg 16.1 shots/rally. 59.7% are epic (12+ shots). Long rallies have 2.3x higher viral potential. Broadcasters and betting platforms need to predict which rallies go long before they do.

headline: #1 coaching need across corpus: 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker' — AI-identified in 5 clips
insight: Top AI-identified improvement areas: ['Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker(5)', 'Vary shot selection to keep opponents guessing(5)', 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points(4)']. Ready-made coaching curriculum. Any platform with this data knows exactly what content to create next.
evidence: Top improvement ops: {'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker': 5, 'Vary shot selection to keep opponents guessing': 5, 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points': 4}
